free_text: Measurements of the Neutron Activation Cross Sections
for Bi and Co at 386 Mev
Ring cyclotron at the Research Center
for Nuclear Physics (RCNP) , Osaka University
1-cm-thick natLi target, bombarded with 389-
MeV protons with average beam intensity of  1 mu-A.
The neutron spectra at 0 and 25 degree were measured
by TOF method using NE213 liquid scintillator.
The spectrum was devided into peak neutrons in the
range of 300-400 MeV (average of 386 MeV) and the
low-energy neutron in the range of 3-300 MeV.
The fluence of high-energy peak neutrons is
Quasi- monoenergetic neutron energy spectra for 246 and
389 MeV 7Li(p,n) reactions at angles from 0 to 30 deg.
Irradiation time 31.6 h.
The samples were measured repeatedly to aid
identification of radioactive species by their
half-lives.
Corrections to the data were made for:
- beam current fluctuations;
- peak eff. of the HPGe det. using the EGS4 code.
- low-energy neutrons by subtracting normalized
activity of the samples irradiated at 25 degree from
the one measured at 0 degree.
Estimated from the following partial
uncertainties:
counting statistics.
HPGe detector efficiency 10%
NE213 detector efficiency 15%
beam current monitoring 5 %
correction for contribution of low-energy
tail 10 %.
Tbl. 2 of Rad. Prot. Dos. 161(2014)139.
